column8: My great-grandmother Lola never ceases to amaze me. She's 95 years old but she's still alive and kicking. Not only does she take care of herself without any help, she___________a boarding house in the Philippines basically all on her own.
column9: How is that possible, you ask? That's an excellent question. My family and I make it back to the Philippines once a year. We always______________up the subject of retirement with her.
column10: My mus says, "Why don't you let someone else run the place. Lola? You know, sit back, relax and put your___________up more often."
column11: "No; that would spell the end of me" she replies. "I dont take a day off work even if I come __________with the flu.Staying busy is the secret to longevity."
column12: She doesn't do all the chores round the boarding house, mind you. She has employees who handle a lot of the heavy lifting, such as washing bed sheets and cleaning rooms. She's more or less in_____________of the money and she makes sure everyone gets paid. She knows what's been done and what hasn't. Her mind is still as sharp as a nail.
column13: So, retiring is completely out of the_________for Lola.
column14: "my last day of work will be the last day of my life; she says. I hope I can__________up with that place when I reach her age.
